title: Reading and Use of English - Part 2
description: For questions 9 - 16, read the text below and think of the word which best fits each gap. Use only one word in each gap. There is an example at the beginning (0).

content.text: The medieval Gothic cathedral represents one of the most ambitious architectural achievements in European history, (0) which expressed both the technological ambitions of its builders and the spiritual aspirations of the communities that funded it. No other building type so completely defined the cultural landscape of the medieval period. (9) Although the term Gothic was originally coined as an insult by Renaissance critics who dismissed earlier medieval styles, it has long since lost any pejorative connotation. (10) Such was the ambition of the master builders who designed these structures that their solutions to problems of height and weight continued to influence architects for centuries. The introduction of the flying buttress was crucial to this achievement, and (11) Provided that sufficient financial resources were available, builders could now erect walls of unprecedented height, pierced by enormous stained-glass windows. Not (12) only did this technical innovation transform the appearance of church architecture, but it also created entirely new possibilities for flooding interiors with coloured light. (13) Regardless of local variations in style across France, England and Germany, the Gothic cathedral shared a commitment to verticality and the overwhelming of the human senses. Not (14) until the Renaissance began to assert different architectural values did the enthusiasm for pointed arches and ribbed vaulting gradually decline. (15) Once a cathedral community had committed to such a vast undertaking, construction might continue for generations, with styles evolving as the building grew. This gives many Gothic cathedrals their characteristic visual variety. (16) Whether approached as structures of faith, engineering achievements or masterpieces of decorative art, medieval cathedrals remain among the most compelling built environments in the world.

example.text: The medieval Gothic cathedral represents one of the most ambitious architectural achievements in European history, (0) which expressed both the technological ambitions of its builders and the spiritual aspirations of the communities that funded it.

questions.explanation: Concessive conjunction used to introduce a contrast or concession between two clauses.
Emphatic determiner in the inverted structure 'Such was/is the [noun] that...' to express a high degree.
Conditional conjunction meaning 'on the condition that', used to introduce a condition and its result.
Part of the correlative conjunction 'Not only... but also', which requires subject-auxiliary inversion after 'Not only'.
Part of the fixed prepositional phrase 'Regardless of', meaning 'without being affected by'.
Part of the 'Not until' negative fronting structure, which requires subject-auxiliary inversion.
Temporal conjunction meaning 'as soon as' or 'after a particular point in time', introducing a sequence of events.
Conjunction introducing two alternatives or possibilities in a summarising conclusion.
